yaml.title: Overview
yaml.description: Practical guidance for open and reproducible research

markdown.markdown:
Welcome. This site supports the **Open Research & Reproducibility** workshop for **Great Ormond Street Institute of Child Health** researchers and staff.  
It is designed to be **practical**, **proportionate**, and to **signpost authoritative guidance** where more detail is needed.

::: callout-note

PhD researchers, early career researchers, postdoctoral researchers, PIs, and professional staff who want research that is **transparent**, **reusable**, and **fit for scrutiny**.
:::

::: callout-tip
## What this course covers

- Structuring projects to ensure data, code, and decisions are **traceable** and **reusable**.
- Applying **FAIR principles** proportionately, including when data cannot be shared openly.
- Documenting research clearly using **READMEs**, **data dictionaries**, **ELNs**, and **SOPs**.
- Improving **code quality** and **reproducibility** without over-engineering.
- Publishing research outputs with appropriate **identifiers**, **licences**, and **availability statements**.
.
:::

## How to use this site

1. Work through **Chapters 1–10** in order (approximately 15–25 minutes per chapter).  
2. Use **Resources and Further Reading** for templates, checklists, and UCL-specific guidance.  
3. Apply the practices to one live project as you go (your project README is the anchor).  
4. Use **Chapter 11** to consolidate learning and identify next steps.

## Chapters

- [Chapter 1 - Why Open?](01-why-open.qmd)  
- [Chapter 2 - FAIR basics](02-fair-basics.qmd)  
- [Chapter 3 - Project organisation and continuity](03-project-mgmt.qmd)  
- [Chapter 4 — Data Management Plans (UCL)](04-dmp.qmd)  
- [Chapter 5 - Organising and tidying data](05-organise-tidy.qmd)  
- [Chapter 6 — Documentation, ELNs & SOPs](06-docs-eln-sops.qmd)  
- [Chapter 7 — Code quality & testing](07-code-quality-testing.qmd)  
- [Chapter 8 — Responsible & sustainable research](08-s-responsibility.qmd)  
- [Chapter 9 — Automation & simple workflows](09-automation.qmd)  
- [Chapter 10 — Publishing, PIDs & preparing for reuse](10-publishing-pids.qmd)  
- [Chapter 11 — Wrap-up & next steps](11-wrap-up.qmd)  

::: callout-important
## Feedback and corrections

If you spot an error or want to suggest an improvement, please contact joana.cruz@ucl.ac.uk.
:::

## Licence and citation

Content is **CC BY 4.0** unless otherwise stated. Please cite original sources linked in each chapter.  
If this site has a DOI (or includes a `CITATION.cff`), add it below.

```text
Suggested citation:
Open Research & Reproducibility (PPP · UCL ICH). Version <v>, <year>.
CC BY 4.0. Available at: <site URL or DOI>
```
